titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 1201

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

p.cl: Thomas, prior, and the chapter of St Cuthbert of the church of Durham, to all Holy Mother Church's sons present and future, greeting.

us to have given, conceded, and by this our charter to have made firm, to Robert son of Goze, our clerk, the church of Ednam with the entire land and all the tofts and crofts belonging to the same church, and with the three chapels of the same church, that is to say, the chapel of Stichill, the chapel of Nenthorn, and the chapel of Newton, and with all benefices belonging to the same church, to be held of Saint Cuthbert and us for his life, freely and quitly from all service that pertains to us, except that he shall give to us yearly as recognition sixteen merks at three terms, 5 merks within the octave of the Purification of Saint Mary, 5 merks within the octave of Saint John the Baptist, and 6 merks within the octave of Saint Cuthbert in September. And he shall discharge all episcopal dues and customs, and when the prior or monks of Durham shall pass through there he shall provide suitable lodging for them and provide for their needs. And if the dedication of the chapels should have happened, he shall cover the expenditure. These things have I conceded, however, to the same Robert, so long as he shall maintain the aforesaid agreement. The same Robert has indeed sworn that he shall faithfully maintain and not question the aforesaid agreement by any craft or trickery so that we are the loser in respect of the same church. For as long as Goze, his father, shall live and shall wish to be in the secular world, he shall have custody of the said Rodbert and the same church; but if Robert should have died before the said Goze, his father, Goze shall hold the church by the same agreement. Robert has provided as pledge to this agreement Abbot Osbert and the community of Jedburgh.

With these witnesses: John, archdeacon; Master Thomas de Vaux; Robert de Fribois; Simon de Camera; John de Raine; Ingram, Elias, and W., the bishop's clerks; R., priest of Selkirk; Dolfin, priest; William, priest; and Sigmund, priest.
